---
title: "Suburbanisation Takes Over Mexico"
subtitle: "How 69 cities grew at the edges while their centres emptied out — and what it costs"
author:
  - name: Gonzalo G. Peraza-Mues
    affiliations:
      - name: Tecnológico de Monterrey, Center for the Future of Cities
      - name: Tecnológico de Monterrey, School of Government and Public Transformation
    email: ggperaza@gmail.com
    corresponding: true
  - name: Eugen Resendiz
    affiliations:
      - name: Tecnológico de Monterrey, Center for the Future of Cities
      - name: Tecnológico de Monterrey, School of Architecture, Art and Design
  - name: Rodolfo Figueroa-Soriano
    affiliations:
      - name: Tecnológico de Monterrey, Center for the Future of Cities
  - name: Rafael Prieto-Curiel
    affiliations:
      - name: Complexity Science Hub, Vienna, Austria
  - name: Roberto Ponce-Lopez
    affiliations:
      - name: Tecnológico de Monterrey, Center for the Future of Cities
date: today
format:
  html:
    theme: default
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    toc-title: "On this page"
    toc-location: right
    smooth-scroll: true
    fig-align: center
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    number-sections: false
    lightbox: true
    embed-resources: false
abstract: |
  Between 1990 and 2020, Mexico's urban population nearly doubled — yet across all
  69 of its metropolitan areas, the historic city centres *lost* residents. As people
  moved outward, central zones shed **2.5 million people**, the share of city-dwellers
  living centrally fell **from over 40% to just 22%**, and the average distance from
  the centre grew **28%** beyond what compact growth would have produced. This is a
  plain-language summary of the study, its method, and why it matters for how cities work.
keywords:
  - urban scaling
  - urban sprawl
  - depopulation
  - Mexico
  - city centres
citation:
  type: article-journal

Highlights:
- We analyzed urban growth dynamics in 69 Mexican metropolitan areas from 1990 to 2020 using radial scaling analysis.
- Most of Mexico's metropolitan areas nearly doubled in population, but their historic centers emptied out; at the same time, people moved from downtown to the peripheries of these cities.
- Between 1990 and 2020, 2.5 million residents left city centers; in detail, the downtown's share of population dropped from 40% to just 22%, meaning that the proportion of people living in the central areas was cut in half.
- Across the 69 cities, the average distance to the city center increased by 28%, lengthening commutes and straining infrastructure.
---

> Cities are supposed to be busiest at their heart. In Mexico, the opposite happened.
> Over three decades of booming growth, people drained out of city centres and piled up
> on the fringes — in *every single one* of the country's 69 large metropolitan areas.

::: {.callout-note}
## The numbers at a glance

- **69** metropolitan areas — all showing the same pattern
- **−2.5 million** residents lost from city centres (1990–2020)
- **22%** now live centrally — down from over 40%
- **+28%** longer distances to the centre, on average
- **4.7 million** people displaced vs. density-preserving growth
:::

## The paradox: booming cities, emptying centres

The world is becoming urban. The number of people living in cities nearly doubled between
1990 and 2020, and is projected to grow another 60% by 2050. Cities can absorb that growth
two ways: by building **up and in** (getting denser) or by spreading **out** (expanding their
footprint). In recent decades, most growth has gone outward — often in a fragmented, unplanned
sprawl that lengthens journeys, strains services, and deepens inequality.

Mexico is a striking case. Its population climbed from **81.7 million in 1990 to 126 million
in 2020**, and the fraction living in cities rose from **71% to 81%** — so the urban population
nearly doubled. Standard urban economics predicts that density should be highest at the centre,
where jobs and services concentrate. Yet Mexico's cities did the reverse: their cores thinned out
while their edges thickened.

The three largest metros make the paradox concrete. Each grew enormously overall, but each lost
people near the middle:

- **Monterrey** grew by 99%, yet the population within 5 km of its centre fell from **485,000 to 377,000**.
- **Guadalajara** grew by 71%, but its inner 5 km dropped from **866,000 to 626,000**.
- **Mexico City** grew by 38% while its central area lost **166,000 residents**.

This study set out to measure that "hollowing-out" precisely — and to explain it — using census
data for all 69 metropolitan areas across four censuses (1990, 2000, 2010, 2020).

## How we measured it

Counting people by distance from the centre sounds simple, but two problems get in the way:
cities come in wildly different sizes, and the shape of a city's population is hard to pin to any
tidy formula. The study introduces two ideas to get around this.

::: {.column-page}
![**The toolkit at a glance.** Each city's population is placed on a fine grid, then read off in
concentric rings around its historic centre (left). Two "remoteness" rescalings shrink a small
city (Colima) and a large one (Guadalajara) onto a common yardstick — a city of one million
people — so their density profiles can be compared directly (right).](assets/figures/summary_methods.png){#fig-methods fig-alt="A multi-panel methodology figure: concentric rings over a gridded city map with labelled remoteness zones; population-density curves; and a demonstration of remoteness scaling that stretches a small city and compresses a large city onto a common one-million-person scale."}
:::

### Remoteness: one yardstick for every city

To compare a giant like Mexico City (21 million) with a small city like Colima on equal terms,
the authors rescale distance into **remoteness** ($r$):

$$r = 1000\,\frac{s}{\sqrt{P}}$$

where $s$ is the distance from the centre and $P$ is the city's population. In plain terms,
remoteness re-expresses every distance as if the city had exactly **one million inhabitants** —
a universal ruler that keeps density values intact. Two people equally "remote" sit at the same
relative position in their cities, regardless of size. That lets the study sort every
neighbourhood into four zones:

| Zone | Remoteness | Meaning |
|------|:----------:|---------|
| **Central** | $r < 3$ | The historic heart, where the city was founded |
| **Intermediate** | $3 \le r < 5$ | Established neighbourhoods, transitioning outward |
| **Distant** | $5 \le r < 9.3$ | Areas built up in later stages |
| **Peri-urban** | $r \ge 9.3$ | The outermost edge of the urban fabric |

### The urban expansion factor: how far the map stretched

The study also tracks the **radial population distribution** $\rho(s)$ — the chance that a randomly
chosen resident lives at a given distance from the centre. The central discovery is that a city's
distribution at a later date is simply a **stretched copy** of its earlier one:

$$\rho(s, t_j) = \frac{1}{\Phi_{ij}}\,\rho\!\left(\frac{s}{\Phi_{ij}},\, t_i\right)$$

The single number $\Phi_{ij}$ — the **urban expansion factor** — says how much the population map
has been pulled outward (or, rarely, pulled in) between two dates. It needs no assumptions about the
"correct" shape of a city, which is exactly where older models fail — especially near the centre,
where the most important changes are happening.

## What we found

### 1. Every core lost people — and got thinner {#finding-1}

Between 1990 and 2020, **all 69 metropolitan areas lost population in their central zone**
($r < 3$), even as every one of them grew overall. Nationwide, central areas shed **2.5 million
residents**, while the intermediate, distant, and peri-urban zones gained **5.8, 16.1, and 11.7
million** people respectively. The share of city-dwellers living centrally was more than halved —
from **over 40% in 1990 to about 22% in 2020**.

Here is **every one of the 69 metropolitan areas** — brown/red cells lost people (near the black-dot
centres), blue cells gained them (out at the edges). **Click any city** to enlarge its map, then
**hover a ring-zone** (central, intermediate, distant, or peri-urban) to read how that zone's
population changed between 1990 and 2020. Under each enlarged map, a chart traces how population
**density** shifted with distance from the centre — the orange line is the city you picked, the bold
black line the national trend.

::: {.column-screen-inset}

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
figure2grid = {
  const cities = await FileAttachment("assets/data/city_maps.json").json();
  const density = await FileAttachment("assets/data/density_change.json").json();
  const vmax = await FileAttachment("assets/data/map_vmax.json").json();

  const zoneKeys = ["central", "intermediate", "distant", "periurban"];
  const zoneMeta = {
    central:      { label: "Central",      range: "r < 3" },
    intermediate: { label: "Intermediate", range: "3 ≤ r < 5" },
    distant:      { label: "Distant",      range: "5 ≤ r < 9.3" },
    periurban:    { label: "Peri-urban",   range: "r ≥ 9.3" }
  };
  // overlay geometry — fraction of image size, identical for every city (measured)
  const R = { r3: 0.150, r5: 0.250, r93: 0.465 };
  const LOSS = "#c65a43", GAIN = "#4e8ab2", HILITE = "rgba(20,61,87,0.18)";
  const fmt = n => n.toLocaleString("en-US");

  const byCve = new Map(cities.map(c => [c.cve, c]));
  let selected = "09.1.01"; // Ciudad de México

  const container = document.createElement("div");
  container.style.cssText = "position:relative;display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:24px;justify-content:center;align-items:flex-start;font-family:system-ui,sans-serif;";
  const left = document.createElement("div");
  left.style.cssText = "flex:0 1 860px;min-width:280px;";
  const right = document.createElement("div");
  right.style.cssText = "flex:0 1 400px;min-width:280px;position:sticky;top:20px;";
  container.append(left, right);

  // ---- grid of 69 thumbnails (7 wide) ----
  const grid = document.createElement("div");
  grid.style.cssText = "display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fill, minmax(76px, 1fr));gap:6px;max-width:860px;";
  const cardByCve = new Map();
  for (const c of cities) {
    const btn = document.createElement("button");
    btn.type = "button";
    btn.title = c.name;
    btn.style.cssText = "display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:2px;padding:3px;border:2px solid transparent;border-radius:8px;background:#fff;cursor:pointer;font:inherit;";
    const im = document.createElement("img");
    im.src = c.thumb; im.alt = c.name; im.loading = "lazy";
    im.style.cssText = "width:100%;aspect-ratio:1;object-fit:cover;border-radius:5px;background:#f3f5f7;";
    const nm = document.createElement("span");
    nm.textContent = c.name;
    nm.style.cssText = "font-size:8.5px;line-height:1.1;color:#555;text-align:center;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap;";
    btn.append(im, nm);
    btn.addEventListener("click", () => selectCity(c.cve));
    cardByCve.set(c.cve, btn);
    grid.append(btn);
  }
  left.append(grid);

  // ---- detail panel: enlarged map + zone overlay ----
  const heading = document.createElement("h3");
  heading.style.cssText = "font-size:1.15rem;margin:0 0 8px;font-weight:700;";
  const mapBox = document.createElement("div");
  mapBox.style.cssText = "position:relative;width:100%;max-width:400px;aspect-ratio:1;border:1px solid #e2e2e2;border-radius:8px;overflow:hidden;background:#fff;";
  const dImg = document.createElement("img");
  dImg.style.cssText = "position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%;";
  dImg.loading = "lazy";

  const NS = "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg";
  const svg = document.createElementNS(NS, "svg");
  svg.setAttribute("viewBox", "0 0 1 1");
  svg.setAttribute("preserveAspectRatio", "none");
  svg.style.cssText = "position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%;";
  const circ = r => `M ${0.5 - r} 0.5 a ${r} ${r} 0 1 0 ${2 * r} 0 a ${r} ${r} 0 1 0 ${-2 * r} 0 Z`;

  // (1) highlight layer — exact zone shapes (evenodd), NOT interactive; tinted on hover
  const shapes = {
    central: circ(R.r3),
    intermediate: circ(R.r5) + " " + circ(R.r3),
    distant: circ(R.r93) + " " + circ(R.r5),
    periurban: "M0 0 H1 V1 H0 Z " + circ(R.r93)
  };
  const hl = {};
  for (const z of zoneKeys) {
    const p = document.createElementNS(NS, "path");
    p.setAttribute("d", shapes[z]);
    p.setAttribute("fill", "transparent");
    p.setAttribute("fill-rule", "evenodd");
    p.setAttribute("pointer-events", "none");
    hl[z] = p;
    svg.append(p);
  }

  // (2) hit surface — ONE transparent rect covering the map; the zone is computed from
  // the cursor's fractional distance from the centre (reliable; avoids per-shape SVG
  // hit-testing, which mis-routes stacked transparent shapes in some browsers).
  const hitRect = document.createElementNS(NS, "rect");
  hitRect.setAttribute("x", 0); hitRect.setAttribute("y", 0);
  hitRect.setAttribute("width", 1); hitRect.setAttribute("height", 1);
  hitRect.setAttribute("fill", "transparent");
  hitRect.setAttribute("pointer-events", "all");
  hitRect.style.cursor = "crosshair";
  let curZone = null;
  function zoneAt(e) {
    const r = mapBox.getBoundingClientRect();
    const dx = (e.clientX - r.left) / r.width - 0.5;
    const dy = (e.clientY - r.top) / r.height - 0.5;
    const rad = Math.hypot(dx, dy);
    return rad < R.r3 ? "central" : rad < R.r5 ? "intermediate" : rad < R.r93 ? "distant" : "periurban";
  }
  function clearZone() { if (curZone) { hl[curZone].setAttribute("fill", "transparent"); curZone = null; } }
  hitRect.addEventListener("mousemove", (e) => {
    const z = zoneAt(e);
    if (z !== curZone) { clearZone(); curZone = z; hl[z].setAttribute("fill", HILITE); showTip(z); }
    moveTip(e);
  });
  hitRect.addEventListener("mouseleave", () => { clearZone(); tip.style.opacity = 0; });
  svg.append(hitRect);

  mapBox.append(dImg, svg);

  const cap = document.createElement("div");
  cap.style.cssText = "font-size:12.5px;color:#666;margin-top:8px;line-height:1.45;";
  const hint = document.createElement("div");
  hint.style.cssText = "font-size:11.5px;color:#999;margin-top:6px;";
  hint.textContent = "Hover a ring-zone for its population change. Zones are scale-normalized, so the rings sit at the same place on every map.";

  // ---- per-city colour-scale bar (the map's shading extent, people per grid cell) ----
  const cbar = document.createElement("div");
  cbar.style.cssText = "margin-top:8px;";
  const cbarGrad = document.createElement("div");
  cbarGrad.style.cssText = "height:12px;border-radius:3px;border:1px solid #e2e2e2;" +
    "background:linear-gradient(90deg,#7a2618,#c65a43,#f5f5f5,#4e8ab2,#173a5e);";
  const cbarRow = document.createElement("div");
  cbarRow.style.cssText = "display:flex;justify-content:space-between;font-size:10.5px;margin-top:2px;";
  const cbLo = document.createElement("span"); cbLo.style.color = LOSS; cbLo.style.fontWeight = "600";
  const cbMid = document.createElement("span"); cbMid.style.color = "#888"; cbMid.textContent = "0";
  const cbHi = document.createElement("span"); cbHi.style.color = GAIN; cbHi.style.fontWeight = "600";
  cbarRow.append(cbLo, cbMid, cbHi);
  const cbCap = document.createElement("div");
  cbCap.style.cssText = "font-size:10.5px;color:#999;text-align:center;margin-top:1px;";
  cbCap.textContent = "population change per grid cell — loss ← → gain (scaled per city)";
  cbar.append(cbarGrad, cbarRow, cbCap);

  // ---- density-change line plots (paper Fig 3): toggle local σ vs. average σ̄ density ----
  const HL_DENS = "#e6550d";
  let densMode = "point";   // "point" = local density σ ; "avg" = average density σ̄ within r
  const densPanel = document.createElement("div");
  densPanel.style.cssText = "margin-top:16px;";
  const densTitle = document.createElement("div");
  densTitle.style.cssText = "font-size:12.5px;font-weight:600;color:#333;margin-bottom:5px;";
  densTitle.textContent = "Density change, 1990 → 2020";
  densPanel.append(densTitle);

  // toggle: local (point) vs average density
  const densToggle = document.createElement("div");
  densToggle.style.cssText = "display:inline-flex;border:1px solid #ccc;border-radius:5px;overflow:hidden;margin-bottom:5px;";
  const modeBtn = {};
  for (const [mode, label] of [["point", "Local"], ["avg", "Average"]]) {
    const b = document.createElement("button");
    b.textContent = label;
    b.style.cssText = "border:none;background:#fff;color:#555;padding:3px 13px;font:500 11px system-ui,sans-serif;cursor:pointer;";
    b.onclick = () => { densMode = mode; drawDensity(); };
    modeBtn[mode] = b;
    densToggle.append(b);
  }
  const densExplain = document.createElement("div");
  densExplain.style.cssText = "font-size:10.5px;color:#999;margin-bottom:2px;";
  densPanel.append(densToggle, densExplain);

  const densCap = document.createElement("div");
  densCap.style.cssText = "font-size:11.5px;color:#999;margin-top:4px;line-height:1.4;";

  const DW = 400, DH = 208, dm = { top: 8, right: 12, bottom: 30, left: 48 };
  const diw = DW - dm.left - dm.right, dih = DH - dm.top - dm.bottom;
  const dx = d3.scaleLinear().domain([0, 10]).range([dm.left, DW - dm.right]);
  const dy = d3.scaleLinear().domain([-6000, 4000]).range([DH - dm.bottom, dm.top]);

  const dsvg = d3.create("svg")
    .attr("viewBox", `0 0 ${DW} ${DH}`).attr("width", "100%")
    .style("max-width", DW + "px").style("height", "auto")
    .style("font-family", "system-ui, sans-serif");
  dsvg.append("defs").append("clipPath").attr("id", "dens-clip").append("rect")
    .attr("x", dm.left).attr("y", dm.top).attr("width", diw).attr("height", dih);
  dsvg.append("g").attr("transform", `translate(0,${DH - dm.bottom})`)
    .call(d3.axisBottom(dx).ticks(6).tickSize(-dih))
    .call(g => g.select(".domain").remove())
    .call(g => g.selectAll(".tick line").attr("stroke", "#000").attr("stroke-opacity", 0.06))
    .call(g => g.selectAll(".tick text").attr("font-size", 9).attr("fill", "#666"));
  dsvg.append("g").attr("transform", `translate(${dm.left},0)`)
    .call(d3.axisLeft(dy).ticks(5).tickSize(-diw).tickFormat(d3.format("~s")))
    .call(g => g.select(".domain").remove())
    .call(g => g.selectAll(".tick line").attr("stroke", "#000").attr("stroke-opacity", 0.06))
    .call(g => g.selectAll(".tick text").attr("font-size", 9).attr("fill", "#666"));
  dsvg.append("line").attr("x1", dm.left).attr("x2", DW - dm.right)
    .attr("y1", dy(0)).attr("y2", dy(0)).attr("stroke", "#888").attr("stroke-dasharray", "3 3");
  dsvg.append("text").attr("x", dm.left + diw / 2).attr("y", DH - 4).attr("text-anchor", "middle")
    .attr("font-size", 10).attr("fill", "#555").text("remoteness  r");
  const dyLabel = dsvg.append("text").attr("transform", "rotate(-90)")
    .attr("x", -(dm.top + dih / 2)).attr("y", 11)
    .attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("font-size", 10).attr("fill", "#555");

  const dContent = dsvg.append("g").attr("clip-path", "url(#dens-clip)");
  const densLine = new Map();
  for (const cve of Object.keys(density.point.cities)) {
    densLine.set(cve, dContent.append("path")
      .attr("fill", "none").attr("stroke", "#bbb").attr("stroke-width", 1).attr("stroke-opacity", 0.22));
  }
  const natPath = dContent.append("path").attr("fill", "none").attr("stroke", "#111").attr("stroke-width", 2.5);
  densPanel.append(dsvg.node(), densCap);

  // (re)draw all density lines for the current mode, then re-apply the selection highlight
  function drawDensity() {
    const ds = density[densMode];
    const cityLine = d3.line().x((v, i) => dx(ds.r[i])).y(v => dy(v));
    for (const [cve, p] of densLine) p.attr("d", cityLine(ds.cities[cve]));
    const nx = ds.national.x || ds.r;
    natPath.attr("d", d3.line().x((v, i) => dx(nx[i])).y(v => dy(v))(ds.national.y));
    dyLabel.text(densMode === "point" ? "Δσ (people/km²)" : "Δσ̄ (people/km²)");
    densExplain.textContent = densMode === "point"
      ? "density right at distance r (each ring)"
      : "average density of everything within distance r";
    for (const m of ["point", "avg"]) {
      const on = m === densMode;
      modeBtn[m].style.background = on ? "#143d57" : "#fff";
      modeBtn[m].style.color = on ? "#fff" : "#555";
    }
    highlightDens();
  }

  // highlight the selected city's density line (orange), keep the national trend on top
  function highlightDens() {
    for (const [, p] of densLine) p.attr("stroke", "#bbb").attr("stroke-width", 1).attr("stroke-opacity", 0.22);
    const dsel = densLine.get(selected);
    if (dsel) dsel.attr("stroke", HL_DENS).attr("stroke-width", 2.5).attr("stroke-opacity", 1).raise();
    natPath.raise();
    const nm = byCve.get(selected);
    densCap.innerHTML =
      `<span style="color:${HL_DENS};font-weight:600">${nm ? nm.name : ""}</span> highlighted · ` +
      `faint grey = other cities · <span style="font-weight:700">black</span> = national trend`;
  }

  right.append(heading, mapBox, cbar, cap, densPanel, hint);

  // ---- tooltip ----
  const tip = document.createElement("div");
  tip.style.cssText = "position:absolute;pointer-events:none;background:#fff;border:1px solid #ccc;border-radius:6px;padding:7px 10px;font-size:12.5px;line-height:1.4;box-shadow:0 4px 14px rgba(0,0,0,.14);opacity:0;transition:opacity .1s;z-index:30;max-width:240px;";
  container.append(tip);
  function distText(z, c) {
    const k = c.km || {};
    if (z === "central") return k.r3 != null ? `within ${k.r3} km` : "";
    if (z === "intermediate") return (k.r3 != null && k.r5 != null) ? `${k.r3}–${k.r5} km` : "";
    if (z === "distant") return (k.r5 != null && k.r9_3 != null) ? `${k.r5}–${k.r9_3} km` : "";
    return k.r9_3 != null ? `beyond ${k.r9_3} km` : "";
  }
  function showTip(z) {
    const c = byCve.get(selected), zn = c.zones[z], m = zoneMeta[z];
    const d = zn.p2020 - zn.p1990;
    const pct = zn.p1990 > 0 ? Math.round(d / zn.p1990 * 100) : null;
    const col = d < 0 ? LOSS : GAIN, sign = d < 0 ? "−" : "+";
    const dist = distText(z, c);
    tip.innerHTML =
      `<strong>${m.label}</strong> <span style="color:#888">(${m.range}${dist ? " · " + dist : ""})</span><br>` +
      `1990: ${fmt(zn.p1990)}<br>2020: ${fmt(zn.p2020)}<br>` +
      `<span style="color:${col};font-weight:600">Change: ${sign}${fmt(Math.abs(d))}` +
      `${pct != null ? ` (${sign}${Math.abs(pct)}%)` : ""}</span>`;
    tip.style.opacity = 1;
  }
  function moveTip(e) {
    const r = container.getBoundingClientRect();
    tip.style.left = (e.clientX - r.left + 14) + "px";
    tip.style.top = (e.clientY - r.top + 14) + "px";
  }

  // ---- selection ----
  function selectCity(cve) {
    selected = cve;
    const c = byCve.get(cve);
    heading.textContent = c.name;
    dImg.src = c.detail;
    dImg.alt = `Population change map for ${c.name}, 1990 to 2020`;
    cap.innerHTML = "Population change by grid cell, 2020 minus 1990. Black dot: city centre; brown/red = loss near the core, blue = gains in the periphery. Dashed rings mark the remoteness zones.";
    for (const [cc, btn] of cardByCve) btn.style.borderColor = cc === cve ? "#143d57" : "transparent";
    // per-city colour-scale numbers (map shading runs to ±vmax people per grid cell)
    const vm = vmax[cve];
    cbLo.textContent = vm != null ? "−" + fmt(vm) : "";
    cbHi.textContent = vm != null ? "+" + fmt(vm) : "";
    highlightDens();  // re-highlight this city's density line (same mode)
    tip.style.opacity = 0;
  }
  drawDensity();       // draw the density lines for the default (local) mode
  selectCity(selected);

  return container;
}
```

:::

The same story shows up in the numbers below — switch between raw population and each zone's share,
and compare what actually happened against a **density-preserving "what-if."**

**Where Mexico's city-dwellers live has flipped.** Population of all 69 metros by remoteness zone
across four censuses (aggregated across every city above). Use **View** to overlay the
**counterfactual** — how the population *would* have been distributed if average density had held
constant — or to show the **difference**: the crowd pushed out of the centre and toward the edge.
Use **Show** to switch between totals and each zone's share.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// population (millions) by remoteness zone: obs = observed; cf = density-preserving counterfactual
// (1990 radial density stretched by √P, renormalized to each year's total). Aggregates reproduced
// from the analysis repo's scripts/analysis_cfactual.py (obs matches outputs/rem_brackets_agg.csv).
popData = [
  {year: 1990, zone: "Central",      obs: 17.7315, cf: 17.7315},
  {year: 2000, zone: "Central",      obs: 17.1807, cf: 18.7815},
  {year: 2010, zone: "Central",      obs: 15.6916, cf: 19.4536},
  {year: 2020, zone: "Central",      obs: 15.2757, cf: 19.9485},
  {year: 1990, zone: "Intermediate", obs: 12.4768, cf: 12.4768},
  {year: 2000, zone: "Intermediate", obs: 16.4278, cf: 16.2743},
  {year: 2010, zone: "Intermediate", obs: 17.7070, cf: 18.9273},
  {year: 2020, zone: "Intermediate", obs: 18.2936, cf: 21.2605},
  {year: 1990, zone: "Distant",      obs:  8.1330, cf:  8.1330},
  {year: 2000, zone: "Distant",      obs: 13.5655, cf: 12.3845},
  {year: 2010, zone: "Distant",      obs: 19.3712, cf: 16.5000},
  {year: 2020, zone: "Distant",      obs: 24.1966, cf: 20.9256},
  {year: 1990, zone: "Peri-urban",   obs:  2.7923, cf:  2.7923},
  {year: 2000, zone: "Peri-urban",   obs:  5.3504, cf:  5.0841},
  {year: 2010, zone: "Peri-urban",   obs:  9.7085, cf:  7.5975},
  {year: 2020, zone: "Peri-urban",   obs: 14.4875, cf: 10.1188}
]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof view1 = Inputs.radio(["Observed", "With counterfactual", "Difference"], {value: "Observed", label: "View"})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof measure1 = Inputs.radio(["Total (millions)", "Share of population (%)"], {value: "Total (millions)", label: "Show"})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
{
  const zones = ["Central", "Intermediate", "Distant", "Peri-urban"];
  const years = [1990, 2000, 2010, 2020];
  const isDiff = view1 === "Difference";
  const isShare = !isDiff && measure1.startsWith("Share");
  const totals = new Map(years.map(y => [y, d3.sum(popData.filter(d => d.year === y), d => d.obs)]));
  const rows = popData.map(d => {
    const sc = isShare ? 100 / totals.get(d.year) : 1;
    return { ...d, obsV: d.obs * sc, cfV: d.cf * sc, diff: d.obs - d.cf };
  });
  const unit = isShare ? "%" : " million";
  const marks = [Plot.ruleY([0])];
  if (isDiff) {
    marks.push(Plot.barY(rows, {
      fx: "zone", x: "year", y: "diff", fill: d => String(d.year), tip: true,
      title: d => `${d.zone} — ${d.year}\n${d.diff >= 0 ? "+" : "−"}${Math.abs(d.diff).toFixed(1)} million vs. counterfactual`
    }));
  } else {
    marks.push(Plot.barY(rows, {
      fx: "zone", x: "year", y: "obsV", fill: d => String(d.year), tip: true,
      title: d => `${d.zone} — ${d.year}\n${d.obsV.toFixed(1)}${unit}`
    }));
    if (view1 === "With counterfactual")
      marks.push(Plot.tickY(rows, { fx: "zone", x: "year", y: "cfV", stroke: "#c0392b", strokeWidth: 2.2 }));
  }
  const plot = Plot.plot({
    height: 380,
    marginBottom: 40,
    marginLeft: 58,
    style: { fontFamily: "system-ui, sans-serif", fontSize: "12px" },
    x: { axis: null },
    fx: { domain: zones, label: null, padding: 0.2 },
    y: {
      label: isDiff ? "↑ Difference vs. counterfactual (millions)"
           : isShare ? "↑ Share of population (%)" : "↑ Population (millions)",
      grid: true, nice: true
    },
    color: { legend: true, domain: years.map(String), range: ["#cdd9e0", "#8fb0c4", "#4e7d9c", "#0f3041"] },
    marks
  });
  const cap = document.createElement("div");
  cap.style.cssText = "font-size:12.5px;color:#666;font-style:italic;margin-top:6px;line-height:1.45;max-width:640px;";
  cap.innerHTML = isDiff
    ? "Observed <strong>minus</strong> the constant-density counterfactual, by zone and year. Negative in the centre (people missing), positive in the periphery (excess) — about <strong>4.7&nbsp;million</strong> pushed out of the core by 2020."
    : view1 === "With counterfactual"
    ? "<span style=\"color:#c0392b;font-weight:600\">Red caps</span> mark where the population would sit under the constant-density counterfactual. A bar short of its cap (the centre) means people were displaced outward; a bar above its cap (the periphery) is the excess crowd."
    : "Colours run light (1990) to dark (2020). The Central block shrinks over time; every outer block grows.";
  const div = document.createElement("div");
  div.append(plot, cap);
  return div;
}
```

It is not just head-counts — density fell too. Near the centre ($r \le 3$), some cities lost as much
as **4,000 people per km²**, with a national average loss of about **1,000 people per km²**. Further
out, density rose. Most of the central thinning happened **before 2010**, during the fastest period
of population growth and the peak of a housing boom. You can see this in the curves beneath each
city's map: nearly all dip below zero near the centre and rise further out, crossing over at a
remoteness of about **2–4** (the bold black line is the national trend).

The pattern is not a handful of cherry-picked cases — it holds everywhere. You can call up any
individual city's own map, and see where it sits among all 69, in the [interactive figure
below](#finding-2).

### 2. Cities grow by stretching — a simple model {#finding-2}

Remarkably, a city's later population map can be recovered by taking its earlier map and simply
**stretching it outward**. The single number that measures that stretch is the **urban expansion
factor $\Phi$** — how far the population distribution has been pulled away from the centre between two
dates. It is **estimated empirically** for every city and period: measured from how much the earlier
distribution must be rescaled to land on the later one — a remarkably tight fit, typically
$R^2 \approx 0.99$ — rather than assumed from any "correct" city shape. Formally, a city's radial
population distribution $\rho(s)$ at a later date is its earlier distribution **stretched by $\Phi$**:

$$\rho(s, t_j) = \frac{1}{\Phi_{ij}}\,\rho\!\left(\frac{s}{\Phi_{ij}},\, t_i\right)$$

Try it yourself: pick a city and **drag the expansion factor $\Phi$**. The blue 1990 curve stretches
outward as $\Phi$ grows; when you reach that city's empirically estimated $\hat\Phi$ (marked on the
slider), the stretched curve lands almost exactly on the faded observed-2020 curve. This one number
captures three decades of change.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
stretchDemo = {
  const rho = await FileAttachment("assets/data/radial_density.json").json();
  const cityList = await FileAttachment("assets/data/city_maps.json").json();

  const C1990 = "#8fb0c4", C2020 = "#0f3041", CANIM = "#e6550d";
  const byName = new Map(cityList.filter(c => rho[c.cve]).map(c => [c.name, c.cve]));
  const names = Array.from(byName.keys()).sort((a, b) => a.localeCompare(b, "es"));

  const container = document.createElement("div");
  container.style.cssText = "max-width:640px;font-family:system-ui,sans-serif;";

  // ---- city dropdown ----
  const ctl = document.createElement("div");
  ctl.style.cssText = "margin-bottom:4px;";
  const sel = document.createElement("select");
  sel.style.cssText = "font:13px system-ui;padding:3px 6px;border:1px solid #ccc;border-radius:5px;";
  for (const n of names) { const o = document.createElement("option"); o.value = n; o.textContent = n; sel.append(o); }
  const selLabel = document.createElement("label");
  selLabel.style.cssText = "font-size:13px;color:#333;display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:6px;";
  selLabel.append("City:", sel);
  ctl.append(selLabel);
  container.append(ctl);

  // ---- Φ slider with empirical-Φ̂ marker ----
  const sliderWrap = document.createElement("div");
  sliderWrap.style.cssText = "position:relative;margin:20px 0 2px;";
  const markRow = document.createElement("div");
  markRow.style.cssText = "position:relative;height:26px;";
  const marker = document.createElement("div");
  marker.style.cssText = "position:absolute;transform:translateX(-50%);text-align:center;bottom:-2px;font-size:11px;color:#c0392b;font-weight:600;line-height:1.05;white-space:nowrap;pointer-events:none;";
  markRow.append(marker);
  const slider = document.createElement("input");
  slider.type = "range"; slider.step = "0.01";
  slider.style.cssText = "width:100%;accent-color:#e6550d;display:block;margin:0;";
  const sliderTicks = document.createElement("div");
  sliderTicks.style.cssText = "display:flex;justify-content:space-between;font-size:10.5px;color:#999;margin-top:1px;";
  const phiReadout = document.createElement("div");
  phiReadout.style.cssText = "font:600 13px system-ui;color:#333;margin-top:5px;";
  sliderWrap.append(markRow, slider, sliderTicks, phiReadout);
  container.append(sliderWrap);

  // ---- chart ----
  const W = 640, H = 300, m = { top: 12, right: 14, bottom: 34, left: 40 };
  const iw = W - m.left - m.right, ih = H - m.top - m.bottom;
  const x = d3.scaleLinear().range([m.left, W - m.right]);
  const y = d3.scaleLinear().range([H - m.bottom, m.top]);
  const svg = d3.create("svg").attr("viewBox", `0 0 ${W} ${H}`).attr("width", "100%")
    .style("max-width", W + "px").style("height", "auto").style("font-family", "system-ui, sans-serif");
  svg.append("defs").append("clipPath").attr("id", "stretch-clip").append("rect")
    .attr("x", m.left).attr("y", m.top).attr("width", iw).attr("height", ih);
  const gx = svg.append("g").attr("transform", `translate(0,${H - m.bottom})`);
  const gy = svg.append("g").attr("transform", `translate(${m.left},0)`);
  svg.append("text").attr("x", m.left + iw / 2).attr("y", H - 3).attr("text-anchor", "middle")
    .attr("font-size", 11).attr("fill", "#555").text("remoteness  r  (scaled distance from centre)");
  svg.append("text").attr("transform", "rotate(-90)").attr("x", -(m.top + ih / 2)).attr("y", 13)
    .attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("font-size", 11).attr("fill", "#555").text("population density  ρ");
  const content = svg.append("g").attr("clip-path", "url(#stretch-clip)");
  const pathFor = (xs, ys) => d3.line().x((_, i) => x(xs[i])).y((_, i) => y(ys[i]))(ys);
  const p2020 = content.append("path").attr("fill", "none").attr("stroke", C2020).attr("stroke-width", 3.5).attr("stroke-opacity", 0.22);
  const p1990 = content.append("path").attr("fill", "none").attr("stroke", C1990).attr("stroke-width", 1.8);
  const panim = content.append("path").attr("fill", "none").attr("stroke", CANIM).attr("stroke-width", 2.6);

  // legend
  const legend = document.createElement("div");
  legend.style.cssText = "display:flex;gap:16px;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:11.5px;color:#555;margin-top:4px;";
  const legItem = (color, text, op) => {
    const s = document.createElement("span"); s.style.cssText = "display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:5px;";
    s.innerHTML = `<span style="display:inline-block;width:16px;height:3px;background:${color};opacity:${op || 1};border-radius:2px;"></span>${text}`;
    return s;
  };
  legend.append(legItem(C1990, "1990"), legItem(CANIM, "1990 stretched by Φ"), legItem(C2020, "2020 (observed)", 0.3));
  container.append(svg.node(), legend);

  // ---- state + drawing ----
  let cur = null, phiHat = 1, sMin = 0.8, sMax = 3;

  function redraw(phi) {
    p1990.attr("d", pathFor(cur.y1990.x, cur.y1990.y));
    p2020.attr("d", pathFor(cur.y2020.x, cur.y2020.y));
    const ax = cur.y1990.x.map(v => v * phi), ay = cur.y1990.y.map(v => v / phi);
    panim.attr("d", pathFor(ax, ay));
    if (+slider.value !== phi) slider.value = phi;
    const near = Math.abs(phi - phiHat) < 0.02;
    phiReadout.innerHTML = `Φ = <span style="color:#e6550d">${phi.toFixed(2)}</span>` +
      (near ? ` &nbsp;<span style="color:#2e7d32;font-weight:600">✓ matches the observed 2020 curve</span>` : "");
  }

  function setCity(name) {
    const rec = rho[byName.get(name)];
    cur = { y1990: rec.years["1990"], y2020: rec.years["2020"] };
    phiHat = rec.G["2020"];
    sMin = 0.8; sMax = Math.min(4, Math.max(2.5, phiHat + 1));
    slider.min = sMin; slider.max = sMax; slider.value = 1;
    x.domain([0, rec.xcap * 1.02]);   // same ~99%-population cutoff as the phase-space density panel
    y.domain([0, Math.max(d3.max(cur.y1990.y) / sMin, d3.max(cur.y2020.y)) * 1.08]);
    gx.call(d3.axisBottom(x).ticks(7).tickSize(-ih)).call(g => g.select(".domain").remove())
      .call(g => g.selectAll(".tick line").attr("stroke", "#000").attr("stroke-opacity", 0.06))
      .call(g => g.selectAll(".tick text").attr("font-size", 9).attr("fill", "#666"));
    gy.call(d3.axisLeft(y).ticks(5).tickSize(-iw).tickFormat(() => "")).call(g => g.select(".domain").remove())
      .call(g => g.selectAll(".tick line").attr("stroke", "#000").attr("stroke-opacity", 0.06));
    sliderTicks.innerHTML = `<span>Φ = ${sMin.toFixed(1)}</span><span>${sMax.toFixed(1)}</span>`;
    const pct = Math.max(0, Math.min(100, (phiHat - sMin) / (sMax - sMin) * 100));
    marker.style.left = pct + "%";
    marker.innerHTML = `Φ&#770; = ${phiHat.toFixed(2)}<br>▾`;
    redraw(1);
  }

  sel.addEventListener("change", () => setCity(sel.value));
  slider.addEventListener("input", () => redraw(+slider.value));
  sel.value = "Colima-Villa de Álvarez";
  setCity(sel.value);
  return container;
}
```

How big is $\Phi$? Across all cities and census periods, it follows a clean rule: expansion is a
**power of population growth**, times a **steady outward drift** that happens even without growth:

$$\Phi_{ij} = \left(\frac{P(t_j)}{P(t_i)}\right)^{\beta} \, e^{\,\alpha\,(t_j - t_i)}$$

Fitting this to 65 of the 69 cities gives **$\beta = 0.60$** (95% CI 0.54–0.67), **$\alpha = 0.0057$
per year** (95% CI 0.0043–0.0071), and an $R^2$ of **0.82**. The two pieces have clear meanings:

- The **$\beta$ term** is *extensive* expansion — the part driven by more people needing more room.
- The **$\alpha$ term** is *intensive* expansion — a steady spreading-out that continues **even if the
  population never grows**, driven by housing policy, cheap peripheral land, and car-friendly
  development.

A city that grew while **keeping its density** would have $\beta = 0.5$ and $\alpha = 0$. Mexico's
values sit *above both* thresholds — the signature of a nation expanding **while losing density**.
(For comparison, $\alpha$ alone amounts to a location 10 km out in 1990 drifting to **11.8 km by
2020**, with no population growth at all.)

The interactive figure below places **every city in every decade** on this map. The horizontal axis
is how much a city's population grew; the vertical axis is its expansion factor $\Phi$. Three
boundaries — growth $= 1$, $\Phi = 1$, and the constant-density diagonal $\Phi = \sqrt{\text{growth}}$ —
carve the plane into **six regimes** that combine density loss/gain, urban expansion/compression, and
population growth/loss; the **solid red curve is the fitted trend** for the Mexican system. **Pick a
city from the menu or click any point** to watch how its population spread outward over three decades
— the animated density panel on the right — and trace its path through the regimes; **scroll to zoom
and drag to pan** into the dense cluster.

::: {.column-page}

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
figure6 = {
  const data = await FileAttachment("assets/data/figure6_points.csv").csv({ typed: true });
  const rdens = await FileAttachment("assets/data/radial_density.json").json();

  const periodColors = { "1990–2000": "#E6AB04", "2000–2010": "#B85A0D", "2010–2020": "#878372" };
  const periods = ["1990–2000", "2000–2010", "2010–2020"];

  const byCity = d3.group(data, d => d.city_name);
  const cities = Array.from(byCity.keys()).sort((a, b) => a.localeCompare(b, "es"));
  const cityCve = new Map(Array.from(byCity, ([n, rows]) => [n, rows[0].cve_code]));
  let selected = "Ciudad de México";

  // ---- geometry (margins baked into the scale ranges so d3.zoom rescaling is exact) ----
  const W = 640, H = 470, m = { top: 22, right: 22, bottom: 50, left: 56 };
  const iw = W - m.left - m.right, ih = H - m.top - m.bottom;
  const x = d3.scaleLinear().domain([0, 3.5]).range([m.left, W - m.right]);
  const y = d3.scaleLinear().domain([0, 2.25]).range([H - m.bottom, m.top]);
  const XMAX = x.domain()[1], YMAX = y.domain()[1];

  // ---- container ----
  const container = document.createElement("div");
  container.style.cssText = "position:relative;display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:22px;align-items:flex-start;font-family:system-ui,sans-serif;";
  const left = document.createElement("div");
  left.style.cssText = "flex:1 1 540px;min-width:300px;";
  const right = document.createElement("div");
  right.style.cssText = "flex:1 1 320px;min-width:250px;";
  container.append(left, right);

  // ---- controls ----
  const controls = document.createElement("div");
  controls.style.cssText = "display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:10px 18px;align-items:center;margin-bottom:8px;";
  const lbl = document.createElement("label");
  lbl.style.cssText = "font-size:14px;font-weight:600;display:flex;gap:8px;align-items:center;";
  lbl.append("Select a city:");
  const select = document.createElement("select");
  select.style.cssText = "font:inherit;padding:4px 8px;border-radius:6px;border:1px solid #bbb;background:#fff;";
  for (const c of cities) {
    const o = document.createElement("option");
    o.value = c; o.textContent = c;
    select.append(o);
  }
  lbl.append(select);
  const resetBtn = document.createElement("button");
  resetBtn.type = "button";
  resetBtn.textContent = "Reset view";
  resetBtn.style.cssText = "font:inherit;font-size:13px;padding:4px 10px;border-radius:6px;border:1px solid #bbb;background:#f6f6f6;cursor:pointer;";
  controls.append(lbl, resetBtn);
  const legend = document.createElement("div");
  legend.style.cssText = "display:flex;gap:14px;font-size:12.5px;color:#333;";
  for (const p of periods) {
    const it = document.createElement("span");
    it.style.cssText = "display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:5px;";
    it.innerHTML = `<span style="width:11px;height:11px;border-radius:50%;background:${periodColors[p]};display:inline-block;"></span>${p}`;
    legend.append(it);
  }
  controls.append(legend);
  left.append(controls);

  // ---- svg scaffold ----
  const svg = d3.create("svg")
    .attr("viewBox", `0 0 ${W} ${H}`).attr("width", "100%")
    .style("max-width", W + "px").style("height", "auto")
    .style("font-family", "system-ui, sans-serif").style("cursor", "grab");

  const clipId = "fig6-clip";
  svg.append("defs").append("clipPath").attr("id", clipId).append("rect")
    .attr("x", m.left).attr("y", m.top).attr("width", iw).attr("height", ih);

  const gx = svg.append("g").attr("transform", `translate(0,${H - m.bottom})`);   // x axis (fixed)
  const gy = svg.append("g").attr("transform", `translate(${m.left},0)`);          // y axis (fixed)
  const content = svg.append("g").attr("clip-path", `url(#${clipId})`);            // zoomable

  // ---- the six phase regions (A–F), bounded by growth=1, Φ=1, Φ=√growth ----
  // teal shades = density loss (above √growth); tan/brown = density gain (below).
  const xsL = d3.range(0, 1.0001, 0.02);          // population loss  (growth < 1)
  const xsR = d3.range(1, XMAX + 0.0001, 0.02);   // population growth (growth > 1)
  const regions = [
    { xs:xsR, y0:d=>Math.sqrt(d), y1:()=>YMAX, color:"#bfe0d8",
      lines:["Density loss","Urban expansion","Population growth"], lx:1.22, ly:2.12, anchor:"start" },   // B
    { xs:xsR, y0:()=>1,           y1:d=>Math.sqrt(d), color:"#f2e6c2",
      lines:["Density gain","Urban expansion","Population growth"], lx:XMAX-0.05, ly:1.22, anchor:"end" }, // C
    { xs:xsR, y0:()=>0,           y1:()=>1, color:"#dcc07a",
      lines:["Density gain","Urban compression","Population growth"], lx:XMAX-0.05, ly:0.6, anchor:"end" },// D
    { xs:xsL, y0:()=>1,           y1:()=>YMAX, color:"#6fc0b7",
      lines:["Density loss","Urban expansion","Population loss"], lx:0.05, ly:2.12, anchor:"start" },      // A
    { xs:xsL, y0:d=>Math.sqrt(d), y1:()=>1, color:"#3f8f86",
      lines:["Density loss","Urban compression","Population loss"], lx:0.05, ly:0.8, anchor:"start" },     // E
    { xs:xsL, y0:()=>0,           y1:d=>Math.sqrt(d), color:"#c79a5a",
      lines:["Density gain","Urban compression","Population loss"], lx:0.97, ly:0.42, anchor:"end" },      // F
  ];
  for (const r of regions)
    r.fill = content.append("path").attr("fill", r.color).attr("fill-opacity", 0.22).attr("stroke", "none");

  // boundary lines
  const vLine = content.append("line").attr("stroke", "#8a8a8a").attr("stroke-dasharray", "4 4"); // growth = 1
  const hLine = content.append("line").attr("stroke", "#8a8a8a").attr("stroke-dasharray", "4 4"); // Φ = 1
  const diag  = content.append("path").attr("fill", "none").attr("stroke", "#555")
    .attr("stroke-width", 1.5).attr("stroke-dasharray", "6 4");                                    // Φ = √growth
  // fitted log-linear model for the Mexican system (paper eq. L_factors):
  //   Φ = growth^β · e^(α·Δt), with β=0.60, α=0.0057; Δt=10 yr for these intercensal periods.
  const BETA_FIT = 0.60, ALPHA_FIT = 0.0057, DT_FIT = 10;
  const phiModel = g => Math.pow(g, BETA_FIT) * Math.exp(ALPHA_FIT * DT_FIT);
  const trend = content.append("path").attr("fill", "none").attr("stroke", "#c0392b")
    .attr("stroke-width", 2.2).attr("stroke-opacity", 0.95);                                       // fitted trend

  // region labels (three lines each)
  for (const r of regions) {
    r.label = content.append("text").attr("text-anchor", r.anchor)
      .attr("font-size", 8.7).attr("fill", "#2b2b2b").style("pointer-events", "none");
    r.lines.forEach((ln, i) => r.label.append("tspan")
      .text(ln).attr("dy", i === 0 ? 0 : "1.15em").attr("font-weight", i === 0 ? 700 : 400));
  }
  const diagLabel = content.append("text").attr("font-size", 9).attr("fill", "#555")
    .attr("text-anchor", "end").style("pointer-events", "none").text("constant density  Φ = √growth");
  const trendLabel = content.append("text").attr("font-size", 9).attr("fill", "#c0392b")
    .attr("font-weight", 600).attr("text-anchor", "end").style("pointer-events", "none")
    .text("Mexican trend (β = 0.60, α = 0.0057)");

  // connector across a selected city's three decades (below points)
  const connector = content.append("path").attr("fill", "none").attr("stroke", "#111")
    .attr("stroke-width", 1).attr("stroke-opacity", 0.55).attr("stroke-dasharray", "2 2");

  // points
  const pts = content.append("g").selectAll("circle").data(data).join("circle")
    .attr("r", 4).attr("fill", d => periodColors[d.period])
    .attr("stroke", "#fff").attr("stroke-width", 0.6).attr("opacity", 0.85)
    .style("cursor", "pointer");

  // axis titles + plot frame (fixed)
  svg.append("rect").attr("x", m.left).attr("y", m.top).attr("width", iw).attr("height", ih)
    .attr("fill", "none").attr("stroke", "#ddd");
  svg.append("text").attr("x", m.left + iw / 2).attr("y", H - 10).attr("text-anchor", "middle")
    .attr("font-size", 12.5).attr("fill", "#333").text("Population growth factor  P(t₂)/P(t₁)");
  svg.append("text").attr("transform", "rotate(-90)").attr("x", -(m.top + ih / 2)).attr("y", 15)
    .attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("font-size", 12.5).attr("fill", "#333")
    .text("Urban expansion factor  Φ");

  // ---- redraw for the current (possibly zoomed) scales ----
  let curX = x, curY = y;
  const diagXs = d3.range(0, XMAX + 0.0001, 0.02);
  function redraw(zx, zy) {
    curX = zx; curY = zy;
    gx.call(d3.axisBottom(zx).ticks(7).tickSize(-ih))
      .call(s => s.select(".domain").remove())
      .call(s => s.selectAll(".tick line").attr("stroke", "#000").attr("stroke-opacity", 0.06));
    gy.call(d3.axisLeft(zy).ticks(6).tickSize(-iw))
      .call(s => s.select(".domain").remove())
      .call(s => s.selectAll(".tick line").attr("stroke", "#000").attr("stroke-opacity", 0.06));
    for (const r of regions)
      r.fill.attr("d", d3.area().x(d => zx(d)).y0(d => zy(r.y0(d))).y1(d => zy(r.y1(d)))(r.xs));
    vLine.attr("x1", zx(1)).attr("x2", zx(1)).attr("y1", m.top).attr("y2", H - m.bottom);
    hLine.attr("y1", zy(1)).attr("y2", zy(1)).attr("x1", m.left).attr("x2", W - m.right);
    diag.attr("d", d3.line().x(d => zx(d)).y(d => zy(Math.sqrt(d)))(diagXs));
    trend.attr("d", d3.line().x(d => zx(d)).y(d => zy(phiModel(d)))(diagXs));
    for (const r of regions) {
      r.label.attr("x", zx(r.lx)).attr("y", zy(r.ly));
      r.label.selectAll("tspan").attr("x", zx(r.lx));
    }
    // place each curve label on its line, rotated parallel to the local tangent (in pixel space)
    const labelAlong = (label, f, g0, off) => {
      const px = zx(g0), py = zy(f(g0)), dg = 0.06;
      const ang = Math.atan2(zy(f(g0 + dg)) - zy(f(g0 - dg)), zx(g0 + dg) - zx(g0 - dg)) * 180 / Math.PI;
      label.attr("x", px).attr("y", py).attr("dy", off).attr("transform", `rotate(${ang},${px},${py})`);
    };
    labelAlong(diagLabel, Math.sqrt, 3.3, -5);
    labelAlong(trendLabel, phiModel, 2.95, -5);
    pts.attr("cx", d => zx(d.growth_factor)).attr("cy", d => zy(d.phi));
    const seq = data.filter(d => d.city_name === selected)
      .sort((a, b) => periods.indexOf(a.period) - periods.indexOf(b.period));
    connector.attr("d", d3.line().x(d => zx(d.growth_factor)).y(d => zy(d.phi))(seq));
    pts.filter(d => d.city_name === selected).raise();
  }

  // ---- tooltip ----
  const tip = document.createElement("div");
  tip.style.cssText = "position:absolute;pointer-events:none;background:#fff;border:1px solid #ccc;border-radius:6px;padding:6px 9px;font-size:12.5px;line-height:1.35;box-shadow:0 4px 14px rgba(0,0,0,.12);opacity:0;transition:opacity .1s;z-index:20;";
  container.append(tip);

  pts.on("mouseover", (e, d) => {
      d3.select(e.currentTarget).attr("r", 7).attr("stroke", "#222").attr("stroke-width", 1).raise();
      tip.style.opacity = 1;
      tip.innerHTML = `<strong>${d.city_name}</strong><br>${d.period}<br>growth ${d.growth_factor.toFixed(2)}× · Φ ${d.phi.toFixed(2)}`;
    })
    .on("mousemove", (e) => {
      const r = container.getBoundingClientRect();
      tip.style.left = (e.clientX - r.left + 12) + "px";
      tip.style.top = (e.clientY - r.top + 12) + "px";
    })
    .on("mouseout", (e, d) => {
      const sel = d.city_name === selected;
      d3.select(e.currentTarget).attr("r", sel ? 6.5 : 4)
        .attr("stroke", sel ? "#111" : "#fff").attr("stroke-width", sel ? 1.5 : 0.6);
      tip.style.opacity = 0;
    })
    .on("click", (e, d) => selectCity(d.city_name));

  // ---- zoom & pan ----
  const zoom = d3.zoom().scaleExtent([1, 20])
    .extent([[m.left, m.top], [W - m.right, H - m.bottom]])
    .translateExtent([[m.left, m.top], [W - m.right, H - m.bottom]])
    .on("zoom", (e) => redraw(e.transform.rescaleX(x), e.transform.rescaleY(y)));
  svg.call(zoom).on("dblclick.zoom", null);
  resetBtn.addEventListener("click", () =>
    svg.transition().duration(400).call(zoom.transform, d3.zoomIdentity));

  const hint = document.createElement("div");
  hint.style.cssText = "font-size:11.5px;color:#888;margin-top:4px;";
  hint.textContent = "Scroll to zoom · drag to pan · click a point or use the menu to select a city.";
  left.append(svg.node(), hint);

  // ---- radial-density panel (animated version of paper Figure 5) ----
  const YR = [1990, 2000, 2010, 2020];
  const yrColor = { 1990: "#cdd9e0", 2000: "#8fb0c4", 2010: "#4e7d9c", 2020: "#0f3041" };
  const RW = 384, RH = 236, rm = { top: 10, right: 12, bottom: 36, left: 40 };
  const riw = RW - rm.left - rm.right, rih = RH - rm.top - rm.bottom;

  const rHead = document.createElement("div");
  rHead.style.cssText = "font-size:14px;font-weight:600;color:#222;margin-bottom:1px;";
  const rSub = document.createElement("div");
  rSub.style.cssText = "font-size:12px;color:#777;margin-bottom:6px;line-height:1.4;";
  rSub.textContent = "Population density vs. distance from the centre — how the city spread out over time.";

  const rx = d3.scaleLinear().range([rm.left, RW - rm.right]);
  const ry = d3.scaleLinear().range([RH - rm.bottom, rm.top]);

  const rsvg = d3.create("svg")
    .attr("viewBox", `0 0 ${RW} ${RH}`).attr("width", "100%")
    .style("max-width", RW + "px").style("height", "auto")
    .style("font-family", "system-ui, sans-serif");
  rsvg.append("defs").append("clipPath").attr("id", "rdens-clip").append("rect")
    .attr("x", rm.left).attr("y", rm.top).attr("width", riw).attr("height", rih);
  const rxAxis = rsvg.append("g").attr("transform", `translate(0,${RH - rm.bottom})`);
  const ryAxis = rsvg.append("g").attr("transform", `translate(${rm.left},0)`);
  rsvg.append("text").attr("x", rm.left + riw / 2).attr("y", RH - 4).attr("text-anchor", "middle")
    .attr("font-size", 10).attr("fill", "#555").text("remoteness  r  (scaled distance from centre)");
  rsvg.append("text").attr("transform", "rotate(-90)").attr("x", -(rm.top + rih / 2)).attr("y", 12)
    .attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("font-size", 10).attr("fill", "#555").text("population density  ρ");
  const rContent = rsvg.append("g").attr("clip-path", "url(#rdens-clip)");
  const rPath = {};
  for (const yr of YR) rPath[yr] = rContent.append("path")
    .attr("fill", "none").attr("stroke", yrColor[yr])
    .attr("stroke-width", yr === 2020 ? 2.6 : 1.8).attr("stroke-opacity", 0.95);

  const rLegend = document.createElement("div");
  rLegend.style.cssText = "display:flex;gap:12px;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:11px;color:#555;margin-top:4px;";
  for (const yr of YR) {
    const it = document.createElement("span");
    it.style.cssText = "display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:4px;";
    it.innerHTML = `<span style="display:inline-block;width:15px;height:3px;background:${yrColor[yr]};border-radius:2px;"></span>${yr}`;
    rLegend.append(it);
  }

  const rCtl = document.createElement("div");
  rCtl.style.cssText = "display:flex;align-items:center;gap:10px;margin-top:8px;";
  const playBtn = document.createElement("button");
  playBtn.style.cssText = "border:1px solid #ccc;border-radius:5px;background:#143d57;color:#fff;font:600 12px system-ui,sans-serif;padding:4px 12px;cursor:pointer;flex:none;";
  playBtn.textContent = "▶ Play";
  const slider = document.createElement("input");
  slider.type = "range"; slider.min = "1990"; slider.max = "2020"; slider.step = "1"; slider.value = "1990";
  slider.style.cssText = "flex:1 1 auto;min-width:70px;accent-color:#143d57;";
  const yrReadout = document.createElement("div");
  yrReadout.style.cssText = "font:600 12.5px system-ui,sans-serif;color:#333;flex:none;width:36px;text-align:right;";
  yrReadout.textContent = "1990";
  rCtl.append(playBtn, slider, yrReadout);

  const rCap = document.createElement("div");
  rCap.style.cssText = "font-size:11px;color:#999;margin-top:6px;line-height:1.45;";
  rCap.textContent = "Press play: all four census years start collapsed onto the 1990 curve, then each expands to its true shape and freezes at its year — the footprint stretching outward as the centre thins.";

  right.append(rHead, rSub, rsvg.node(), rLegend, rCtl, rCap);

  // ---- animation state + logic ----
  let curCve = null, rafId = null, playing = false, playFrom = 1990, playT0 = null;
  const PLAY_MS = 4800;

  // cumulative expansion factor at continuous time t (geometric interp between census years)
  function Gat(G, t) {
    if (t <= 1990) return G["1990"];
    if (t >= 2020) return G["2020"];
    const k = Math.min(2, Math.floor((t - 1990) / 10));
    const y0 = 1990 + k * 10, g0 = G[String(y0)], g1 = G[String(y0 + 10)];
    return g0 * Math.pow(g1 / g0, (t - y0) / 10);
  }

  function drawRadial(t) {
    const rec = rdens[curCve];
    if (!rec) return;
    const Gt = Gat(rec.G, t);
    for (const yr of YR) {
      const ys = rec.years[String(yr)], Gy = rec.G[String(yr)];
      const s = (t <= yr ? Gt : Gy) / Gy;          // = G(min(t, yr)) / G(yr)
      rPath[yr].attr("d", d3.line().x((v, i) => rx(ys.x[i] * s)).y(v => ry(v / s))(ys.y));
    }
    slider.value = Math.round(t);
    yrReadout.textContent = Math.round(t);
  }

  function setCityRadial(cve) {
    curCve = cve;
    const rec = rdens[cve];
    if (!rec) return;
    // x-axis capped at the ~99%-population remoteness (flat tail is clipped by the plot's clip-rect);
    // y-axis from the tallest state over the whole sweep (census breakpoints bound the interp states)
    let ym = 0;
    for (const T of YR) {
      const Gt = rec.G[String(T)];
      for (const yr of YR) {
        const ys = rec.years[String(yr)], Gy = rec.G[String(yr)];
        const s = (T <= yr ? Gt : Gy) / Gy;
        ym = Math.max(ym, d3.max(ys.y) / s);
      }
    }
    rx.domain([0, rec.xcap * 1.02]);
    ry.domain([0, ym * 1.06]);
    rxAxis.call(d3.axisBottom(rx).ticks(6).tickSize(-rih))
      .call(g => g.select(".domain").remove())
      .call(g => g.selectAll(".tick line").attr("stroke", "#000").attr("stroke-opacity", 0.06))
      .call(g => g.selectAll(".tick text").attr("font-size", 9).attr("fill", "#666"));
    ryAxis.call(d3.axisLeft(ry).ticks(4).tickSize(-riw).tickFormat(() => ""))
      .call(g => g.select(".domain").remove())
      .call(g => g.selectAll(".tick line").attr("stroke", "#000").attr("stroke-opacity", 0.06));
    playRadial(1990);   // autoplay from 1990 on each city selection
  }

  function stopPlay() {
    if (rafId) cancelAnimationFrame(rafId);
    rafId = null; playing = false; playBtn.textContent = "▶ Play";
  }

  function playRadial(from) {
    stopPlay();
    playFrom = Math.max(1990, Math.min(2019, from));
    playing = true; playT0 = null; playBtn.textContent = "❚❚ Pause";
    const dur = PLAY_MS * (2020 - playFrom) / 30;
    function frame(ts) {
      if (playT0 === null) playT0 = ts;
      const t = playFrom + Math.min(1, (ts - playT0) / dur) * (2020 - playFrom);
      drawRadial(t);
      if (t < 2020 && playing) rafId = requestAnimationFrame(frame);
      else { stopPlay(); drawRadial(2020); }
    }
    rafId = requestAnimationFrame(frame);
  }

  playBtn.onclick = () => {
    if (playing) stopPlay();
    else playRadial(+slider.value >= 2020 ? 1990 : +slider.value);
  };
  slider.addEventListener("input", () => { stopPlay(); drawRadial(+slider.value); });

  // ---- selection ----
  function selectCity(name) {
    selected = name;
    if (select.value !== name) select.value = name;
    rHead.textContent = name;
    setCityRadial(cityCve.get(name));
    pts.attr("opacity", d => d.city_name === name ? 1 : 0.16)
      .attr("r", d => d.city_name === name ? 6.5 : 4)
      .attr("stroke", d => d.city_name === name ? "#111" : "#fff")
      .attr("stroke-width", d => d.city_name === name ? 1.5 : 0.6);
    const seq = data.filter(d => d.city_name === name)
      .sort((a, b) => periods.indexOf(a.period) - periods.indexOf(b.period));
    connector.attr("d", d3.line().x(d => curX(d.growth_factor)).y(d => curY(d.phi))(seq));
    pts.filter(d => d.city_name === name).raise();
  }

  select.addEventListener("change", () => selectCity(select.value));
  redraw(x, y);
  selectCity(selected);

  return container;
}
```

:::

Each city contributes **three points** — one per decade (1990–2000, 2000–2010, 2010–2020). The vast
majority land in the upper **density-loss** region. A handful of tourism-driven ports (Cancún, Los
Cabos, Puerto Vallarta) sit far to the right on runaway growth, while the twin city of
Tlaxcala-Apizaco is the rare case that pulled *inward*.

### 3. How many people were displaced? {#finding-3}

To gauge the human scale, the study asks a "what-if": had cities grown while **holding their average
density constant**, how many fewer people would have been pushed out of the centre? The answer is
stark. Compared with that density-preserving path, **4.7 million people were effectively displaced
from city centres** — nearly **double** the 2.5 million drop measured directly.

You can see this zone by zone in the [population chart above](#finding-1): switch **View** to
**With counterfactual** to overlay the density-preserving path, or to **Difference** to read the gap
directly — negative in the centre (people missing), positive at the edge (the excess crowd).

### 4. Longer distances, higher costs {#finding-4}

Stretching a city's population outward has a direct, unavoidable consequence: the **average distance
to the centre grows**. With Mexico's typical growth factor of **2.16** over 30 years and the fitted
parameters, mean distances end up **28% longer** than under density-preserving growth. Longer
distances mean:

- more vehicle-kilometres, emissions, and air pollution;
- reduced access to water, sewage, schools, and healthcare on the fringe;
- fragmented labour markets and longer commutes;
- deeper car dependency — Mexico's car fleet ballooned from **under 5 million in 1990 to over 40
  million today**.

## Why it happened

The hollowing-out was not an accident of geography; it was engineered by a stack of reinforcing
forces on both sides of the housing market.

**On the demand side**, a demographic boom and rising incomes — helped by remittances and by
**NAFTA (1994)** — created a wave of new households ready to buy homes and, increasingly, to own cars
that made long commutes feasible.

**On the supply side**, two structural reforms opened the fringe:

1. **The 1992 reform of communal ("ejido") land tenure** allowed communal land to be sold, unlocking
   vast tracts of cheap, developable land on the urban edge.
2. **From 2001, the national housing agency became a financial intermediary**, pouring credit into
   large-scale peripheral housing. Annual housing production leapt from about **50,000 units to more
   than 1.4 million** by 2013.

Developers built where land was cheapest — far from the centre — and credit followed. But roads,
transit, water, sewage, and schools rarely kept pace, leaving residents farther out and less well
served.

::: {.callout-important}
## The key policy lever

Some outward growth is unavoidable when a population doubles. But the study isolates a second,
*policy-driven* component — the steady drift ($\alpha$) that occurs **even without population growth**.
That part reflects dysfunctional housing finance and car-oriented planning, not demographic necessity.
Compact design, transit investment, and housing-finance reform could shrink it — and with it, the
density losses and social costs documented here.
:::

## Why it matters

For most of history, cities were densest at their core. Mexico's are now densest several kilometres
out. That inversion — driven by housing markets pushing residents ever outward even as the overall
population grew — is a *self-organising* dynamic that simple models of city growth cannot reproduce.

The framework introduced here offers planners a **model-free, size-independent ruler** for measuring
and comparing expansion across cities and decades. Because it makes no assumption about a city's
"correct" shape, it works precisely where older density models break down — near the centre, where
the most consequential changes unfold. That makes it a practical tool for anticipating the spatial
fallout of population shifts, and for designing growth that is more compact, better served, and more
equitable.

## Data & code

- **Census data:** [INEGI](https://www.inegi.org.mx/) (Mexico's national statistics institute).
- **Processed dataset & code (Zenodo):** [doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20630381](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20630381)
- **Analysis code (GitHub):** [CentroFuturoCiudades/scaling_depopulation_mex](https://github.com/CentroFuturoCiudades/scaling_depopulation_mex)
- **Interactive city maps:** [CitiesMoving.com/UrbanDispersion](https://citiesmoving.com/UrbanDispersion/)

---

*This is a plain-language summary of the research article “Scaling and Population Loss in Mexican Urban
Centres” by G. G. Peraza-Mues, E. Resendiz, R. Figueroa-Soriano, R. Prieto-Curiel, and R. Ponce-Lopez.
Affiliations: Tecnológico de Monterrey (Center for the Future of Cities; School of Government and Public
Transformation; School of Architecture, Art and Design) and the Complexity Science Hub, Vienna. All
figures are from the paper; the two interactive charts are built from the paper's reported values and
model parameters.*
